message:
feat(abi): port robust-futex list + LSM self-attr API (5 syscalls) (bd-92dkc)

Five more Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via
libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno bridge:

  - set_robust_list(*head, len) (SYS_set_robust_list=273) — register
    a per-thread robust-futex list head so the kernel walks it on
    thread exit and releases held mutexes.
  - get_robust_list(pid, **head_ptr, *len_ptr)
    (SYS_get_robust_list=274) — read the registered head + len for
    the given pid (or 0 = self). NULL pointer guards return EFAULT.
  - lsm_get_self_attr(attr_id, *ctx, *size, flags) (Linux 6.8+,
    syscall 459) — read an LSM (SELinux/AppArmor/Smack/...)
    attribute for the calling thread.
  - lsm_set_self_attr(attr_id, *ctx, size, flags) (Linux 6.8+,
    syscall 460) — write an LSM attribute for the calling thread.
  - lsm_list_modules(*ids, *size, flags) (Linux 6.8+, syscall 461)
    — enumerate the LSM modules active in the running kernel.

LSM syscalls are not yet in libc 0.2.185, so their numbers
(459/460/461) are embedded as locals.

Tests: 8 abi tests covering get_robust_list happy-path on the test
runner thread (validates 24-byte size_of struct robust_list_head
matches glibc), NULL pointer guards across all five wrappers, and
ENOSYS/EINVAL/EOPNOTSUPP fallbacks for the LSM calls. The
set_robust_list zero-len round-trip also restores the original
saved head/len so the rest of the test process keeps its own
robust-mutex cleanup hook intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-8zc2","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity nonexistent pid with zero cpusetsize should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_getaffinity(huge_nonexistent_pid, 0, &mask), so zero cpusetsize dominates the nonexistent-pid contract. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:09.327692918Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:13:36.603143380Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:13:36.601016033Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep. Covers historical CVE surface for dotted-quad arithmetic and IPv6 '::' expansion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-92dkc","title":"PORT: set_robust_list / get_robust_list + lsm_get_self_attr / set_self_attr / list_modules","description":"Add 5 more Linux libc syscall wrappers. (1) set_robust_list(*head, len) -> int (SYS_set_robust_list=273) — register a per-thread robust futex list head so the kernel can clean up if the thread exits while holding mutexes. (2) get_robust_list(pid, **head_ptr, *len_ptr) -> int (SYS_get_robust_list=274) — read the registered head + len for the given pid (or 0 = self). (3) lsm_get_self_attr(attr_id, *ctx, *size, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 459) — read an LSM (SELinux/AppArmor/Smack/...) attribute for the calling thread into a buffer, with *size updated. (4) lsm_set_self_attr(attr_id, *ctx, size, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 460) — write an LSM attribute for the calling thread. (5) lsm_list_modules(*ids, *size, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 461) — enumerate LSM modules active in the running kernel. All forward via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno bridge; NULL-pointer guards return EFAULT where the kernel would otherwise UB.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:22:40.670259200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:22:40.670259200Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.577129952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.574907623Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity EAI_ALLDONE semantics for synchronous-degenerate gai_cancel/gai_suspend inputs and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95zf","title":"AI_ADDRCONFIG review: null-node IPv6-only and dual-stack coverage","description":"Review of cod's bd-4qqu fix (commit 8996ad1 hoisting AI_ADDRCONFIG filter out of Some(text) branch) found 2 coverage gaps in the null-node test set: (1) IPv6-only interface state — the null-node IPv4 filter case is tested but the symmetric IPv6-only case is not, so an asymmetric filter bug would escape. (2) dual-stack interface state with null-node — no test verifies both AF_INET and AF_INET6 nodes are preserved when both families have non-loopback routes. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/resolv_abi_test.rs additions only, no code changes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:37:35.826818907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.778398472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.776390943Z","close_reason":"Added 2 complementary null-node AI_ADDRCONFIG tests in commit b935235: IPv6-only filter symmetry + dual-stack preservation. Suite now covers all 4 quadrants of the {IPv4,IPv6} x {filter,keep} matrix for null-node under AI_ADDRCONFIG.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-du4q6","title":"PORT: mergesort + heapsort (BSD libc sort variants)","description":"BSD libc mergesort(base, nmemb, size, compar) and heapsort(base, nmemb, size, compar) — sort variants of qsort with the same signature except they return int (0 on success, -1 with errno on failure). mergesort is STABLE (preserves input order for equal elements); heapsort is NOT stable but uses no auxiliary storage. Both are ~50 years old, present in libbsd 0.0/0.3, used by mtree, ls -t, sort -s, and any tool that needs stability without writing its own merge sort. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::sort::{mergesort, heapsort} that operate on byte slices via index permutation (so the existing qsort comparator-callback infrastructure works unchanged) + abi shims that return 0/-1 with ENOMEM on alloc failure (mergesort) or EINVAL on bad inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:23:10.898281655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:32.846694117Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:32.844274737Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::sort {mergesort (stable, copy-out + timsort), heapsort (in-place max-heap)} + stdlib_abi shims with EINVAL/ENOMEM mapping; 13+9 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dvr22","title":"[HIGH] fuzz gap: signal/socket/fortify/setjmp/mmap/dlfcn/pthread/c11threads ABI modules are unfuzzed (mmap now partially covered by fuzz_mmap)","description":"## Motivation

/testing-fuzzing skill discovery pass on crates/frankenlibc-abi/src enumerated 3646 `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` symbols across 38 modules and cross-referenced against the 30 fuzz bins registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. The CVE-class gap is concentrated in 8 modules that currently have **no** fuzz target:

| Priority | Module | Symbols | CVE class |
|---------|--------|---------|----------|
| 1 | mmap | 11 | memory-safety (UAF / OOB / W^X bypass) — **covered by fuzz_mmap in same PR** |
| 2 | signal | 29 | handler setup races, async-signal-safety, privilege escalation |
| 3 | socket | 18 | network attack surface, kernel ABI complexity |
| 4 | fortify | 79 | `__*_chk` overflow-detection wrappers — if broken, they silently fail to protect |
| 5 | setjmp | 12 | control-flow integrity, sigsetjmp edge cases |
| 6 | dlfcn | 7 | dlopen/dlsym attack surface |
| 7 | pthread | 141 | concurrency (mutex/cond/rwlock/barrier) — best done as TSan campaign |
| 8 | c11threads | 21 | C11 threading (thrd_/mtx_/cnd_) — same pattern as pthread |

## Status of priority-1 (mmap)

Delivered in the same push: `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mmap.rs` — stateful harness with a live-mapping arena, 7 ops (Map/Unmap/Protect/Sync/Advise/Remap/MarkStaleInPlace), guard-byte sentinels on writable mappings, alignment invariant on mmap/mremap return addresses, and the use-after-unmap contract (every op on a Stale slot must return -1 / MAP_FAILED). The harness intentionally exercises PROT_EXEC / RWX requests against the hardened-mode membrane to cover the expected refuse-or-clamp path. Registered as `fuzz_mmap` bin. Validation: cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings both clean.

## Fix direction for the remaining 7

Each should follow the pattern already established by the 5 fuzz targets cc-libc shipped earlier this session (fuzz_printf_adversarial, fuzz_pathname, fuzz_env, fuzz_sched, fuzz_pidfd_timer):

- Stateful archetype with a handle table where applicable (signal → signal handler slots; socket → sockfds; pthread → thread / mutex / cond handles; setjmp → jmp_buf storage slots).
- Return-code contract + lifecycle invariant (stale handle → must fail).
- Direct syscall differential where possible (signal, socket via libc::syscall). For pthread/c11threads, use a BTreeMap / counter shadow model because the ABI deviates from raw syscall shape.
- Safety bounds: signal restricted to {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} the way fuzz_pidfd_timer does; socket in PF_LOCAL with SOCK_DGRAM + AT_EMPTY_PATH style so no real network traffic.
- TSan-specific campaign for pthread/c11threads; run with `RUSTFLAGS=-Zsanitizer=thread`.

## Score priority

Per /testing-fuzzing skill, score = (untrusted-input) * (complexity) * (unsafe/native) * (prior CVEs). The mmap surface scores highest because of 40+ years of mmap/munmap/mprotect CVEs (CVE-2017-1000405 ptrace race, CVE-2016-5195 'Dirty COW' mprotect interaction, CVE-2023-40283 vma-use-after-free). Signal is second (CVE-2020-14386 af_packet, CVE-2021-44733 tee), fortify third (CVE-2023-4911 'Looney Tunables' via __stack_chk_* interaction).

## Exit criteria

One child bead per remaining module, each:
- Delivers a fuzz target in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/
- Registers it in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
- Achieves ≥1000 exec/s (skill Hard Rule 1) or, for stateful harnesses, ≥100 exec/s
- Cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings clean
- Seed corpus committed (exclude artifacts per existing .gitignore)
","notes":"Fuzz coverage delivered for all 8 priority modules + 4 pthread sub-targets: fuzz_mmap, fuzz_signal, fuzz_socket, fuzz_fortify, fuzz_setjmp, fuzz_dlfcn, fuzz_pthread_mutex, fuzz_pthread_cond, fuzz_pthread_keys, fuzz_pthread_rwlock, fuzz_pthread_sync_misc, fuzz_c11threads. All 12 sources present + registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. Verified via rch full build (exit 0, 62s remote) + fuzz_signal 30s smoke (2.4M runs, ~77k exec/s, 0 crashes). fuzz_xdr added later in this session covers the 9th high-CVE surface (SunRPC XDR) — found and fixed bd-bhqcn, bd-lc5ss, bd-xpgak in that target.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:23:16.085325702Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:33:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T02:33:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","full-review","fuzz","syscall"]}
-{"id":"bd-dyh3u","title":"PORT: statmount/listmount + finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load + kexec_file_load (8 modern Linux syscalls)","description":"Add 8 modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno{,_long} bridges. (1) statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 457) — query a single mount by ID into struct statmount. (2) listmount(*req, *out, count, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 458) — enumerate child mount IDs into a buffer. (3) finit_module(fd, *args, flags) -> int (SYS_finit_module=313) — load a kernel module from an open file descriptor. (4) quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_quotactl_fd=443) — like quotactl but takes an open fd instead of a block-device path. (5) map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) -> long (Linux 6.6+, syscall 453) — allocate a CET shadow stack on x86_64. (6) bpf(cmd, *attr, size) -> int (SYS_bpf=321) — universal BPF interface. (7) kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_load=246) — load a new kernel image for kexec reboot. (8) kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len, *cmdline, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_file_load=320) — same but from open fds. NULL-pointer guards where applicable; opaque pointers for kernel structs whose layouts are version-specific.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:32.663108317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:32.663108317Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-dyh3u","title":"PORT: statmount/listmount + finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load + kexec_file_load (8 modern Linux syscalls)","description":"Add 8 modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno{,_long} bridges. (1) statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 457) — query a single mount by ID into struct statmount. (2) listmount(*req, *out, count, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 458) — enumerate child mount IDs into a buffer. (3) finit_module(fd, *args, flags) -> int (SYS_finit_module=313) — load a kernel module from an open file descriptor. (4) quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_quotactl_fd=443) — like quotactl but takes an open fd instead of a block-device path. (5) map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) -> long (Linux 6.6+, syscall 453) — allocate a CET shadow stack on x86_64. (6) bpf(cmd, *attr, size) -> int (SYS_bpf=321) — universal BPF interface. (7) kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_load=246) — load a new kernel image for kexec reboot. (8) kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len, *cmdline, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_file_load=320) — same but from open fds. NULL-pointer guards where applicable; opaque pointers for kernel structs whose layouts are version-specific.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:32.663108317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:18:28.139183489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:18:28.136792663Z","close_reason":"shipped: 8 modern Linux syscall wrappers (statmount, listmount, finit_module, quotactl_fd, map_shadow_stack, bpf, kexec_load, kexec_file_load) + 10 abi tests covering NULL guards + privilege/kernel fallbacks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dyrgj","title":"PORT: dehumanize_number (NetBSD human-readable size parser)","description":"NetBSD dehumanize_number(str, *size_out) parses a C string of optional whitespace + optional sign + digits + optional one-char suffix (bBkKmMgGtTpPeE for 1024^k multipliers, where b/B = 1) into an int64_t. Returns 0 on success (writes through *size), -1 with errno = EINVAL for malformed input or ERANGE for arithmetic overflow. Used by NetBSD ftpd/mtree/dd-conv and other tools for human-readable size limits in config files. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::dehumanize_number::parse returning Result<i64, DehumanizeError> with Invalid/Overflow variants + thin abi shim that maps the error categories to errno + writes the value through the caller pointer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:44.076867837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:26:55.373009488Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:26:55.371160026Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::dehumanize_number + stdlib_abi shim; 28+10 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e2ct","title":"syscall: close_range invalid flags on reversed bounds should still return EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(5, 4, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags plus reversed bounds stay in the exact EINVAL bucket.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:32.686596433Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.336651943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.334571755Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
